file.content:
name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd  # v6.0.2
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@e97e2d8cc328f1b50210efc529dca0028893a2d9  # stable
        with:
          toolchain: stable
          targets: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu,aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu,aarch64-apple-darwin
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@c19371144df3bb44fab255c43d04cbc2ab54d1c4  # v2.9.1

      - name: Patch version from tag
        run: |
          VERSION="${GITHUB_REF_NAME#v}"
          if ! [[ "$VERSION" =~ ^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
            echo "Tag must be of the form vX.Y.Z (got: ${GITHUB_REF_NAME})"
            exit 1
          fi
          sed -i "0,/^version = \".*\"/{s/^version = \".*\"/version = \"$VERSION\"/}" Cargo.toml
          cargo check

      - name: Cache cargo-zigbuild
        id: cache-zigbuild
        uses: actions/cache@668228422ae6a00e4ad889ee87cd7109ec5666a7  # v5.0.4
        with:
          path: ~/.cargo/bin/cargo-zigbuild
          key: cargo-zigbuild-0.22.1-${{ runner.os }}

      - name: Cache zig pip download
        uses: actions/cache@668228422ae6a00e4ad889ee87cd7109ec5666a7  # v5.0.4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: REDACTED

      - name: Install zig
        run: pip3 install ziglang==0.15.2

      - name: Install cargo-zigbuild
        if: steps.cache-zigbuild.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: cargo install --locked cargo-zigbuild --version 0.22.1

      - name: Build linux-amd64
        run: cargo zigbuild --release --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu

      - name: Build linux-arm64
        run: cargo zigbuild --release --target aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu

      - name: Build darwin-arm64
        run: cargo zigbuild --release --target aarch64-apple-darwin

      - name: Collect binaries
        run: |
          cp target/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/release/ferrous-comics ferrous-comics-linux-amd64
          cp target/aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu/release/ferrous-comics ferrous-comics-linux-arm64
          cp target/aarch64-apple-darwin/release/ferrous-comics ferrous-comics-darwin-arm64

      - uses: softprops/action-gh-release@153bb8e04406b158c6c84fc1615b65b24149a1fe  # v2.6.1
        with:
          files: |
            ferrous-comics-linux-amd64
            ferrous-comics-linux-arm64
            ferrous-comics-darwin-arm64

content.text:
The `softprops/action-gh-release` step only does one thing here: upload 3 files to a release. The `gh` CLI can do that natively with no Node.js runtime involved — and it's already installed on every `ubuntu-latest` runner. That's a cleaner fix than waiting on a third-party action to bump their runtime.

Here's the plan:

input.content:
# Replace softprops/action-gh-release with gh CLI

## Context

`softprops/action-gh-release@v2.6.1` (currently pinned) runs on Node.js 20, which GitHub is deprecating — it will become a hard error on June 2, 2026. The action's Node 24 upgrade is an open issue with no stable release yet.

The action is only used for one thing: uploading 3 binary files to a release. The `gh` CLI (pre-installed on all `ubuntu-latest` runners) does this natively with `gh release upload`, has no Node.js runtime dependency, and is maintained by GitHub itself. Replacing it eliminates the third-party dependency entirely.

## Change

**File:** `.github/workflows/release.yml`

Replace the `softprops/action-gh-release` step:

```yaml
      - uses: softprops/action-gh-release@153bb8e04406b158c6c84fc1615b65b24149a1fe  # v2.6.1
        with:
          files: |
            ferrous-comics-linux-amd64
            ferrous-comics-linux-arm64
            ferrous-comics-darwin-arm64
```

With a `gh release upload` command:

```yaml
      - name: Upload release assets
        run: gh release upload "${{ github.ref_name }}" ferrous-comics-linux-amd64 ferrous-comics-linux-arm64 ferrous-comics-darwin-arm64
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
```

The job already has `permissions: contents: write`, which is what `gh release upload` needs.

## Notes
- `github.ref_name` resolves to the tag name (e.g. `v1.5.0`) on tag pushes — same release the workflow is building for
- `GH_TOKEN` is the standard env var `gh` uses for auth; `github.token` is the built-in repo token with the `contents: write` permission already granted
- No other files change

## Verification
- Push a `v*` tag and confirm the release workflow completes with binaries attached and no Node deprecation warning
- `gh release view <tag>` shows all 3 assets

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Release v1.4.0 Plan

## Context

9 commits have landed on main since v1.3.0 (released 2026-03-22), including one user-facing feature (the `version` subcommand), a major internal refactor (module split), an edition upgrade, and the binary size/CI optimizations from this session. The new feature warrants a minor version bump → **v1.4.0**.

## What's included since v1.3.0

| PR | Change |
|----|--------|
| #24 | Binary size/CI: strip, LTO, size opt; 6.4 MB → 2.2 MB; cache zigbuild in CI |
| #23 | README: remove stale SMTP limitation note and TODO |
| #22 | Upgrade to Rust edition 2024 |
| #21 | **New feature:** `ferrous-comics version` subcommand (prints version + git hash) |
| #20 | Add MIT license |
| #19/#18 | CLAUDE.md documentation improvements |
| #17 | Split `main.rs` into `config`, `db`, `http`, `email` modules |
| –  | Add CI badge to README |

## Proposed release tag: `v1.4.0`

## Proposed release notes

```
## What's new

### Features
- **`version` subcommand** — `ferrous-comics version` now prints the version number and git commit hash baked in at build time.

### Internal
- Codebase reorganized into four modules (`config`, `db`, `http`, `email`) for better maintainability
- Upgraded to Rust edition 2024
- Release binaries are ~66% smaller (6.4 MB → 2.2 MB) via strip, thin LTO, size optimization, and `panic = abort`
- Faster CI: `cargo-zigbuild` and zig pip wheel are now cached between runs
- Dropped unused `regex` feature from `env_logger`

### Documentation
- MIT license
- CI badge in README
- README: removed stale SMTP-only limitation note
```

## Steps to execute

1. Create GitHub release as **draft** with the notes above (no tag yet):
   ```
   gh release create v1.4.0 --draft --title "v1.4.0" --notes "..."
   ```

2. Push the tag to trigger the release workflow (builds binaries):
   ```
   git tag v1.4.0 && git push origin v1.4.0
   ```
   The workflow patches Cargo.toml version from the tag, builds `linux-amd64`, `linux-arm64`, `darwin-arm64`, and attaches them to the existing draft release via `softprops/action-gh-release`.

3. Verify the workflow run completes successfully and binaries are attached.

4. Publish the draft release (remove draft status):
   ```
   gh release edit v1.4.0 --draft=false
   ```

## Critical files
- `.github/workflows/release.yml` — triggered by `v*` tag push, no changes needed
- `Cargo.toml` — version is patched automatically from the tag by the workflow

## Verification
- Release workflow succeeds with all 3 binaries attached
- `gh release view v1.4.0` shows correct notes and assets

content.text:
**Findings:**
- We're already on the latest stable (`v2.6.1`) — nothing newer to update to
- The action is actively maintained (pushed yesterday), but the Node 24 upgrade hasn't landed in a release yet
- 117 open issues, including a recent regression bug

**Recommendation: drop the action entirely.** It's only uploading 3 files. Replacing it with `gh release upload` (one line, GitHub's own CLI, no Node runtime) permanently eliminates the dependency and the deprecation concern.

input.summary: Replace softprops/action-gh-release with a single `gh release upload` command in release.yml — eliminates the Node.js 20 deprecation warning and the third-party dependency entirely
input.plan:
# Replace softprops/action-gh-release with gh CLI

## Context

`softprops/action-gh-release@v2.6.1` (currently pinned) runs on Node.js 20, which GitHub is deprecating — it will become a hard error on June 2, 2026. The action's Node 24 upgrade is an open issue with no stable release yet.

The action is only used for one thing: uploading 3 binary files to a release. The `gh` CLI (pre-installed on all `ubuntu-latest` runners) does this natively with `gh release upload`, has no Node.js runtime dependency, and is maintained by GitHub itself. Replacing it eliminates the third-party dependency entirely.

## Change

**File:** `.github/workflows/release.yml`

Replace the `softprops/action-gh-release` step:

```yaml
      - uses: softprops/action-gh-release@153bb8e04406b158c6c84fc1615b65b24149a1fe  # v2.6.1
        with:
          files: |
            ferrous-comics-linux-amd64
            ferrous-comics-linux-arm64
            ferrous-comics-darwin-arm64
```

With a `gh release upload` command:

```yaml
      - name: Upload release assets
        run: gh release upload "${{ github.ref_name }}" ferrous-comics-linux-amd64 ferrous-comics-linux-arm64 ferrous-comics-darwin-arm64
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
```

The job already has `permissions: contents: write`, which is what `gh release upload` needs.

## Notes
- `github.ref_name` resolves to the tag name (e.g. `v1.5.0`) on tag pushes — same release the workflow is building for
- `GH_TOKEN` is the standard env var `gh` uses for auth; `github.token` is the built-in repo token with the `contents: write` permission already granted
- No other files change

## Verification
- Push a `v*` tag and confirm the release workflow completes with binaries attached and no Node deprecation warning
- `gh release view <tag>` shows all 3 assets

toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd  # v6.0.2
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@e97e2d8cc328f1b50210efc529dca0028893a2d9  # stable
        with:
          toolchain: stable
          targets: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu,aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu,aarch64-apple-darwin
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@c19371144df3bb44fab255c43d04cbc2ab54d1c4  # v2.9.1

      - name: Patch version from tag
        run: |
          VERSION="${GITHUB_REF_NAME#v}"
          if ! [[ "$VERSION" =~ ^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
            echo "Tag must be of the form vX.Y.Z (got: ${GITHUB_REF_NAME})"
            exit 1
          fi
          sed -i "0,/^version = \".*\"/{s/^version = \".*\"/version = \"$VERSION\"/}" Cargo.toml
          cargo check

      - name: Cache cargo-zigbuild
        id: cache-zigbuild
        uses: actions/cache@668228422ae6a00e4ad889ee87cd7109ec5666a7  # v5.0.4
        with:
          path: ~/.cargo/bin/cargo-zigbuild
          key: cargo-zigbuild-0.22.1-${{ runner.os }}

      - name: Cache zig pip download
        uses: actions/cache@668228422ae6a00e4ad889ee87cd7109ec5666a7  # v5.0.4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: REDACTED

      - name: Install zig
        run: pip3 install ziglang==0.15.2

      - name: Install cargo-zigbuild
        if: steps.cache-zigbuild.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: cargo install --locked cargo-zigbuild --version 0.22.1

      - name: Build linux-amd64
        run: cargo zigbuild --release --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu

      - name: Build linux-arm64
        run: cargo zigbuild --release --target aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu

      - name: Build darwin-arm64
        run: cargo zigbuild --release --target aarch64-apple-darwin

      - name: Collect binaries
        run: |
          cp target/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/release/ferrous-comics ferrous-comics-linux-amd64
          cp target/aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu/release/ferrous-comics ferrous-comics-linux-arm64
          cp target/aarch64-apple-darwin/release/ferrous-comics ferrous-comics-darwin-arm64

      - uses: softprops/action-gh-release@153bb8e04406b158c6c84fc1615b65b24149a1fe  # v2.6.1
        with:
          files: |
            ferrous-comics-linux-amd64
            ferrous-comics-linux-arm64
            ferrous-comics-darwin-arm64

lines: +        run: gh release upload "${{ github.ref_name }}" ferrous-comics-linux-amd64 ferrous-comics-linux-arm64 ferrous-comics-darwin-arm64

```yaml
          cp target/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/release/ferrous-comics ferrous-comics-linux-amd64
          cp target/aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu/release/ferrous-comics ferrous-comics-linux-arm64
          cp target/aarch64-apple-darwin/release/ferrous-comics ferrous-comics-darwin-arm64
      - name: Upload release assets
        run: gh release upload "${{ github.ref_name }}" ferrous-comics-linux-amd64 ferrous-comics-linux-arm64 ferrous-comics-darwin-arm64
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
```

## Context
- Triggered on push to `v*` tags (e.g. v1.5.0)
- The release is pre-created as a draft before the tag is pushed (as established in the release process for this repo)
- `github.ref_name` on a tag push resolves to the tag name (e.g. `v1.5.0`)
- `github.token` has `contents: write` permission via the job-level `permissions` block
- `gh` CLI is pre-installed on ubuntu-latest runners

Questions to investigate:
1. Does `gh release upload` work correctly when the release was pre-created as a draft (before the tag was pushed)?
2. Is `${{ github.ref_name }}` the right way to reference the tag, or should it be `${{ github.ref }}` or `GITHUB_REF_NAME`?
3. Is `GH_TOKEN` the correct env var name for `gh` authentication, or should it be `GITHUB_TOKEN`?
4. Does `gh release upload` fail if the release doesn't exist yet (e.g. if someone forgets to pre-create the draft)?
5. Any shell injection risk in the `run` step?
6. Does `gh` need any additional permissions or configuration beyond GH_TOKEN?

Return real issues found, or confirm correctness.

## Installation

1. Clone this repo
1. Copy `example.env` to `.env` and fill in your SMTP credentials
1. `cargo build --[01;31m[Krelease[m[K`
1. Run: `./target/[01;31m[Krelease[m[K/ferrous-comics`
1. The binary will create `xkcd_comics.db` and (if `XKCD_DOWNLOAD=true`) a `comics/` directory. If `xkcd_history.txt` exists from a previous version it will be automatically migrated and renamed to `xkcd_history.txt.migrated`
1. (optional) Install as a cron job

## Dependencies

* Rust / Cargo

## Configuration

All settings are read from environment variables (or a `.env` file) with the `XKCD_` prefix:

| Variable | Required | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `XKCD_MAIL_TO` | yes | — | Recipient email address (comma-separated for multiple) |
| `XKCD_MAIL_FROM` | yes | — | Sender email address |
| `XKCD_SMTP_SERVER` | yes | — | SMTP hostname |
| `XKCD_SMTP_PORT` | no | `587` | SMTP port |
| `XKCD_SMTP_STARTTLS` | no | `true` | Use STARTTLS |
| `XKCD_SMTP_USERNAME` | no | — | SMTP username |
| `XKCD_SMTP_PASSWORD` | no | — | SMTP password |

`GH_TOKEN`, `GITHUB_TOKEN` (in order of precedence): an authentication token that will be used when
a command targets either `github.com` or a subdomain of `ghe.com`. Setting this avoids being prompted to
authenticate and takes precedence over previously stored credentials.

`GH_ENTERPRISE_TOKEN`, `GITHUB_ENTERPRISE_TOKEN` (in order of precedence): an authentication
token that will be used when a command targets a GitHub Enterprise Server host.

`GH_HOST`: specify the GitHub hostname for commands where a hostname has not been provided, or
cannot be inferred from the context of a local Git repository. If this host was previously
authenticated with, the stored credentials will be used. Otherwise, setting `GH_TOKEN` or
`GH_ENTERPRISE_TOKEN` is required, depending on the targeted host.

`GH_REPO`: specify the GitHub repository in the `[HOST/]OWNER/REPO` format for commands
that otherwise operate on a local repository.

`GH_EDITOR`, `GIT_EDITOR`, `VISUAL`, `EDITOR` (in order of precedence): the editor tool to use
for authoring text.

`GH_BROWSER`, `BROWSER` (in order of precedence): the web browser to use for opening links.

`GH_DEBUG`: set to a truthy value to enable verbose output on standard error. Set to `api`
to additionally log details of HTTP traffic.

`DEBUG` (deprecated): set to `1`, `true`, or `yes` to enable verbose output on standard
error.

`GH_PAGER`, `PAGER` (in order of precedence): a terminal paging program to send standard output
to, e.g. `less`.

`GLAMOUR_STYLE`: the style to use for rendering Markdown. See
<https://github.com/charmbracelet/glamour#styles>

`NO_COLOR`: set to any value to avoid printing ANSI escape sequences for color output.

`CLICOLOR`: set to `0` to disable printing ANSI colors in output.

`CLICOLOR_FORCE`: set to a value other than `0` to keep ANSI colors in output
even when the output is piped.

`GH_COLOR_LABELS`: set to any value to display labels using their RGB hex color codes in terminals that
support truecolor.

`GH_ACCESSIBLE_COLORS` (preview): set to a truthy value to use customizable, 4-bit accessible colors.

`GH_FORCE_TTY`: set to any value to force terminal-style output even when the output is
redirected. When the value is a number, it is interpreted as the number of columns
available in the viewport. When the value is a percentage, it will be applied against
the number of columns available in the current viewport.

`GH_NO_UPDATE_NOTIFIER`: set to any value to disable GitHub CLI update notifications.
When any command is executed, gh checks for new versions once every 24 hours.
If a newer version was found, an upgrade notice is displayed on standard error.

`GH_NO_EXTENSION_UPDATE_NOTIFIER`: set to any value to disable GitHub CLI extension update notifications.
When an extension is executed, gh checks for new versions for the executed extension once every 24 hours.
If a newer version was found, an upgrade notice is displayed on standard error.

`GH_CONFIG_DIR`: the directory where gh will store configuration files. If not specified,
the default value will be one of the following paths (in order of precedence):

Links: [{"title":"gh release upload no longer working for draft releases when used within GH action · Issue #5252 · cli/cli","url":"https://github.com/cli/cli/issues/5252"},{"title":"Uploading an artificat to a draft release doesn't work (not found) · Issue #6198 · cli/cli","url":"https://github.com/cli/cli/issues/6198"},{"title":"Failed `gh release create` with files leaves behind a draft release · Issue #6645 · cli/cli","url":"https://github.com/cli/cli/issues/6645"},{"title":"```gh release create``` successfully returns before release is available to subsequent calls · Issue #6599 · cli/cli","url":"https://github.com/cli/cli/issues/6599"},{"title":"Multiple Draft Releases Created when >100 Releases Exist in GitHub · Issue #602 · softprops/action-gh-release","url":"https://github.com/softprops/action-gh-release/issues/602"},{"title":"`gh release create --draft --prerelease` goes untagged · Issue #11589 · cli/cli","url":"https://github.com/cli/cli/issues/11589"},{"title":"Unable to create/upload/publish because can't update draft · Issue #356 · softprops/action-gh-release","url":"https://github.com/softprops/action-gh-release/issues/356"},{"title":"Reusing existing draft release no longer works in GH org · Issue #163 · softprops/action-gh-release","url":"https://github.com/softprops/action-gh-release/issues/163"},{"title":"File not found after upload · Issue #194 · softprops/action-gh-release","url":"https://github.com/softprops/action-gh-release/issues/194"},{"title":"How to upload a file to an existing draft release? · Issue #323 · softprops/action-gh-release","url":"https://github.com/softprops/action-gh-release/issues/323"}]

Based on the search results, here's what I found about `gh release upload` behavior with draft releases that don't exist:

## Error Behavior

When attempting to upload to a draft release that doesn't exist or cannot be found, `gh release upload` returns the error: "release not found".

## Common Causes

There are several documented scenarios where this error occurs:

1. **API Visibility Issues**: The GitHub API may only list non-draft releases, even with a valid token, which can make draft releases inaccessible to subsequent `gh` commands.

2. **Timing Issues**: The command to create a release may return before the release is available to subsequent calls, leading to errors when trying to upload files right after creating a release.

3. **Draft Release Lookup Failures**: When running sequential commands like `gh release create` with `--draft`, followed by `gh release upload`, the second upload command sometimes fails with "release not found".

## Expected vs Actual Behavior

When `gh release create` returns successfully, it's expected that the release exists for the next call, but this expectation isn't always met in practice, particularly with draft releases in GitHub Actions workflows.

The search results show this has been a persistent issue reported in multiple GitHub issues related to the `gh` CLI and GitHub Actions.

input.prompt: What is the issue with gh release upload on draft releases? Is there a workaround or status update?
What is the error when uploading to a draft release? Is it still an issue?

content.content:
# GitHub CLI Release Upload Issue on Draft Releases

## The Problem

The original issue reported that `gh release upload` failed for draft releases when used in GitHub Actions with the `GITHUB_TOKEN`. The user observed that while `gh release list` could display draft releases using GraphQL, the REST API endpoint used by `gh release upload` returned an empty list of releases.

## Root Cause

The discrepancy stemmed from API differences: "gh release list also lists draft releases (it's using graphql in the background)" while the REST API endpoint queried by upload functionality didn't include unpublished releases in its response.

## Current Status

The issue was closed as resolved. A maintainer noted that "Both GitHub platform and the CLI have shipped many fixes to Releases process since the start of this thread until today." The original reporter later confirmed the problem had been resolved, likely through API-side changes.

## Recommendation

If you're experiencing similar issues, ensure you're using a current version of the GitHub CLI, as the underlying API limitations have been addressed since February 2022.

# GitHub CLI Release Upload Issue - Resolution Summary

## The Error

The error message encountered was: **"release not found"**

This occurred when attempting to upload artifacts to a draft release immediately after creating it using `gh release upload $TAG`.

## Root Cause

According to the issue resolution, there was a synchronization lag between GitHub's GraphQL and REST APIs. The GraphQL API (used by `gh release list`) would show the newly created draft release, but the REST API (used by `gh release upload`) would not immediately recognize it—typically for about 2 seconds after creation.

## Current Status

**The issue is now closed as COMPLETED.** A platform update was deployed to improve consistency between the APIs. However, a small replication lag can still occur.

## Recommended Workarounds

1. **Upload files during creation:** Use `gh release create -p $TAG <files>` to upload artifacts in the same command, which automatically keeps the release as draft until uploads complete.

2. **Add a delay:** Insert a `sleep 3` statement after `gh release create` to allow sufficient time for API synchronization before attempting uploads.

input.prompt: What is the difference between github.ref and github.ref_name? Which should be used for tag pushes?

content.content:
# Difference Between `github.ref` and `github.ref_name`

**`github.ref`** is the fully-formed reference. For tags, it follows the format `refs/tags/<tag_name>`. As stated in the documentation: "For tags it is `refs/tags/<tag_name>`. For example, `refs/heads/feature-branch-1`."

**`github.ref_name`** is the short reference name. The documentation explains: "The short ref name of the branch or tag that triggered the workflow run. This value matches the branch or tag name shown on GitHub. For example, `feature-branch-1`."

**For tag pushes**, use **`github.ref_name`** if you need just the tag name (e.g., `v1.0.0`), or **`github.ref`** if you need the complete reference path (e.g., `refs/tags/v1.0.0`). The choice depends on whether you need the full qualified reference or just the user-friendly name.

I've completed the web search for your query about GitHub Actions `contents: write` permission and `gh release upload`. Here are the key findings:

## Key Results:

**Permission Requirements for Release Uploads:**

The workflow needs `contents: write` permission to publish releases and upload assets. If you are encountering "resource not accessible by integration" errors, you will need to add the `contents: write` permission to the token using granular permissions.

**Using `gh release upload`:**

The GitHub CLI command `gh release upload <tag> <files>...` uploads files to a release with a specific tag. To make the GITHUB_TOKEN have permission to upload to releases, you need `permissions: contents: write`

**Workflow Configuration:**

You can use permissions either as a top-level key to apply to all jobs in the workflow, or within specific jobs, where all actions and run commands within that job that use the GITHUB_TOKEN gain the access rights you specify.

**Security Considerations:**

The `contents: write` permission gives broader permission than needed for releases, as it also allows committing to the repository and merging PRs.

The search results contain practical examples and detailed documentation on implementing this in your GitHub Actions workflows.

input.prompt: What permissions are needed for gh release upload? Does it work with GITHUB_TOKEN in GitHub Actions?

content.content:
# GitHub Release Upload Permissions

Based on the article, uploading assets to releases via `gh release upload` requires the same core permission as creating releases:

**Required Permission:** `contents: write`

The article states that "Uploading Assets: If your workflow uploads assets (e.g., binaries, ZIP files) to the draft release, `contents: write` still suffices. Assets are part of the release object, so no additional scopes are needed."

## How It Works with GITHUB_TOKEN

Yes, `gh release upload` works with `GITHUB_TOKEN` in GitHub Actions when properly configured. You need to explicitly grant the permission at either the workflow or job level:

```yaml
permissions:
  contents: write
```

The article emphasizes using "job-level permissions" to restrict `contents: write` only to the specific job uploading assets, following the principle of least privilege. This approach enhances security by limiting token exposure to just the necessary operations.

content.text:
Excellent. Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile my findings.

---

## ADVERSARIAL REVIEW: gh release upload Workflow Change

I've investigated all six questions systematically. Here are my findings:

### CRITICAL ISSUE FOUND

**Question 1: Draft Release Availability**

**Status: RISK / UNCERTAIN** ⚠️

The change assumes that a pre-created draft release exists and is accessible via `gh release upload`. However, the GitHub issue search revealed a **documented and persistent problem**:

- **Issue #5252** (cli/cli): `gh release upload` failed on draft releases because the REST API endpoint didn't include unpublished drafts in its listing, despite the GraphQL API showing them.
- **Issue #6198** (cli/cli): Upload to draft releases failed with "release not found" due to API synchronization lag between GraphQL and REST endpoints (typically ~2 seconds after creation).

While both issues are marked "resolved," the resolution is vague ("many fixes shipped since February 2022"). **The core concern remains: if the draft release isn't already accessible via the REST API at the time this step runs, the upload will fail.**

**Recommendation:** Either confirm through testing that draft releases created before tag push are immediately accessible, or add a brief sleep/retry after release creation, OR use `gh release create` with `--draft` flags in the same workflow step to ensure atomicity.

---

### Other Questions: VERIFIED CORRECT

**Question 2: `${{ github.ref_name }}` is correct** ✓

According to GitHub Actions documentation, `github.ref_name` correctly resolves to the tag name (e.g., `v1.5.0`) for tag push events. Using `github.ref` would also work but is unnecessarily verbose (`refs/tags/v1.5.0`).

**Question 3: `GH_TOKEN` is correct** ✓

GitHub CLI recognizes both `GH_TOKEN` and `GITHUB_TOKEN` environment variables (in that precedence order). Using `GH_TOKEN` with `${{ github.token }}` is correct and standard.

**Question 4: No pre-existing release handling** ⚠️

The workflow **will fail silently if the release doesn't exist**. `gh release upload` returns exit code 1 and the error message "release not found" goes to stderr, causing the job to fail. This is actually desirable (fail-fast), but the workflow provides no fallback. If the draft creation step is omitted accidentally, the entire CI/CD pipeline fails.

**Question 5: No shell injection risk** ✓

The `${{ github.ref_name }}` is properly quoted (`"${{ github.ref_name }}"`), preventing any shell injection. Even if a tag name contained special characters like `;` or `|`, they'd be safely escaped.

**Question 6: Permissions are correct** ✓

The job-level `permissions: contents: write` is sufficient and correct. According to GitHub's documentation, `contents: write` is the required permission for both uploading assets and managing releases.

---

## Summary

**Real Issue:**
The main concern is whether pre-created draft releases are reliably accessible to `gh release upload` via the GitHub API. Given the documented history of timing/visibility bugs with draft releases, **you should either:**

1. **Test this in a real deployment** before merging to confirm the draft is accessible.
2. **Add retry logic** if failures are observed.
3. **Create the release within the same workflow** (e.g., `gh release create --draft`) instead of pre-creating it.

All other aspects of the change are technically sound.

---

**Sources:**
- [gh release upload no longer working for draft releases · Issue #5252](https://github.com/cli/cli/issues/5252)
- [Uploading to a draft release doesn't work · Issue #6198](https://github.com/cli/cli/issues/6198)
- [What Permissions Does GITHUB_TOKEN Require for GitHub Action Releases?](https://www.codegenes.net/blog/what-permissions-does-github-token-require-for-releases-from-a-github-action/)
- [GitHub Actions contexts - github.ref_name](https://docs.github.com/en/actions/learn-github-actions/contexts#github-context)
- [GitHub CLI environment variables](https://cli.github.com/manual/gh#environment)

toolUseResult.prompt:
Adversarial review of this change to .github/workflows/release.yml. Act as a skeptical reviewer. Look for bugs, correctness problems, and edge cases — not style.

## Diff

```diff
-      - uses: softprops/action-gh-release@153bb8e04406b158c6c84fc1615b65b24149a1fe  # v2.6.1
-        with:
-          files: |
-            ferrous-comics-linux-amd64
-            ferrous-comics-linux-arm64
-            ferrous-comics-darwin-arm64
+      - name: Upload release assets
+        run: gh release upload "${{ github.ref_name }}" ferrous-comics-linux-amd64 ferrous-comics-linux-arm64 ferrous-comics-darwin-arm64
+        env:
+          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
```

## Full context of the job

```yaml
jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@...
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@...
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@...
      - name: Patch version from tag
        run: |
          VERSION="${GITHUB_REF_NAME#v}"
          sed -i ... Cargo.toml
          cargo check
      - [caching steps]
      - [build steps for linux-amd64, linux-arm64, darwin-arm64]
      - name: Collect binaries
        run: |
          cp target/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/release/ferrous-comics ferrous-comics-linux-amd64
          cp target/aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu/release/ferrous-comics ferrous-comics-linux-arm64
          cp target/aarch64-apple-darwin/release/ferrous-comics ferrous-comics-darwin-arm64
      - name: Upload release assets
        run: gh release upload "${{ github.ref_name }}" ferrous-comics-linux-amd64 ferrous-comics-linux-arm64 ferrous-comics-darwin-arm64
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}
```

## Context
- Triggered on push to `v*` tags (e.g. v1.5.0)
- The release is pre-created as a draft before the tag is pushed (as established in the release process for this repo)
- `github.ref_name` on a tag push resolves to the tag name (e.g. `v1.5.0`)
- `github.token` has `contents: write` permission via the job-level `permissions` block
- `gh` CLI is pre-installed on ubuntu-latest runners

Questions to investigate:
1. Does `gh release upload` work correctly when the release was pre-created as a draft (before the tag was pushed)?
2. Is `${{ github.ref_name }}` the right way to reference the tag, or should it be `${{ github.ref }}` or `GITHUB_REF_NAME`?
3. Is `GH_TOKEN` the correct env var name for `gh` authentication, or should it be `GITHUB_TOKEN`?
4. Does `gh release upload` fail if the release doesn't exist yet (e.g. if someone forgets to pre-create the draft)?
5. Any shell injection risk in the `run` step?
6. Does `gh` need any additional permissions or configuration beyond GH_TOKEN?

Return real issues found, or confirm correctness.

That's actually the right fail-fast behavior, but I'll also add `--clobber` so retried workflow runs don't fail on duplicate asset names. The timing issue from old bugs doesn't apply here — the draft is created minutes before the workflow runs.

toolUseResult.newString: run: gh release upload --clobber "${{ github.ref_name }}" ferrous-comics-linux-amd64 ferrous-comics-linux-arm64 ferrous-comics-darwin-arm64
toolUseResult.originalFile:
name: Release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - 'v*'

jobs:
  build:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@de0fac2e4500dabe0009e67214ff5f5447ce83dd  # v6.0.2
      - uses: dtolnay/rust-toolchain@e97e2d8cc328f1b50210efc529dca0028893a2d9  # stable
        with:
          toolchain: stable
          targets: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu,aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu,aarch64-apple-darwin
      - uses: Swatinem/rust-cache@c19371144df3bb44fab255c43d04cbc2ab54d1c4  # v2.9.1

      - name: Patch version from tag
        run: |
          VERSION="${GITHUB_REF_NAME#v}"
          if ! [[ "$VERSION" =~ ^[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ ]]; then
            echo "Tag must be of the form vX.Y.Z (got: ${GITHUB_REF_NAME})"
            exit 1
          fi
          sed -i "0,/^version = \".*\"/{s/^version = \".*\"/version = \"$VERSION\"/}" Cargo.toml
          cargo check

      - name: Cache cargo-zigbuild
        id: cache-zigbuild
        uses: actions/cache@668228422ae6a00e4ad889ee87cd7109ec5666a7  # v5.0.4
        with:
          path: ~/.cargo/bin/cargo-zigbuild
          key: cargo-zigbuild-0.22.1-${{ runner.os }}

      - name: Cache zig pip download
        uses: actions/cache@668228422ae6a00e4ad889ee87cd7109ec5666a7  # v5.0.4
        with:
          path: ~/.cache/pip
          key: REDACTED

      - name: Install zig
        run: pip3 install ziglang==0.15.2

      - name: Install cargo-zigbuild
        if: steps.cache-zigbuild.outputs.cache-hit != 'true'
        run: cargo install --locked cargo-zigbuild --version 0.22.1

      - name: Build linux-amd64
        run: cargo zigbuild --release --target x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu

      - name: Build linux-arm64
        run: cargo zigbuild --release --target aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu

      - name: Build darwin-arm64
        run: cargo zigbuild --release --target aarch64-apple-darwin

      - name: Collect binaries
        run: |
          cp target/x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu/release/ferrous-comics ferrous-comics-linux-amd64
          cp target/aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu/release/ferrous-comics ferrous-comics-linux-arm64
          cp target/aarch64-apple-darwin/release/ferrous-comics ferrous-comics-darwin-arm64

      - name: Upload release assets
        run: gh release upload "${{ github.ref_name }}" ferrous-comics-linux-amd64 ferrous-comics-linux-arm64 ferrous-comics-darwin-arm64
        env:
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ github.token }}

lines: -        run: gh release upload "${{ github.ref_name }}" ferrous-comics-linux-amd64 ferrous-comics-linux-arm64 ferrous-comics-darwin-arm64
+        run: gh release upload --clobber "${{ github.ref_name }}" ferrous-comics-linux-amd64 ferrous-comics-linux-arm64 ferrous-comics-darwin-arm64

## Summary

- Drops `softprops/action-gh-release` entirely — the action runs on Node.js 20, which GitHub is deprecating (hard error June 2, 2026), and the Node 24 upgrade has no stable release yet
- Replaces it with `gh release upload`, which is pre-installed on `ubuntu-latest`, has no Node.js runtime, and is maintained by GitHub itself
- `--clobber` allows clean workflow re-runs without asset name conflicts

## Test plan

- [ ] CI passes on this branch
- [ ] Next release: tag push triggers workflow, all 3 binaries attach to the pre-created draft release, no Node deprecation warning in logs
EOF
